title: Bridge Essay: Schizophrenia Detection Using Convolutional Neural Networks on EEG Data.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Daimon bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**Schizophrenia Detection Using Convolutional Neural Networks on EEG Data.** — Research square (2025). PMID: 41282228. DOI: 10.21203/rs.3.rs-7863978/v1. Score: 31. Abnormal corollary discharge has been implicated in schizophrenia and manifests as reduced suppression of auditory evoked responses during self-generated sounds. We investigate whether deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) trained on EEG from abasic button-tone task can detect schizophrenia at the single-trial/subject level.
